name: Specialty Clinic Cleaning
criticalZone: specialty equipment surfaces, which differ by discipline
centralRisk: equipment contamination where cleaning responsibility is ambiguous
regulatoryFramework: 105 CMR 140 — Massachusetts DPH, Licensure of Clinics
CDC Guidelines for Environmental Infection Control in Health-Care Facilities

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Diagnostic and therapeutic equipment not disinfected after each use in accordance with recognised standards of practice. In Massachusetts this is a clinic licensure requirement, not guidance.
Cleaning compounds not clearly labelled or not stored in a locked utility closet, and no service sink with hot and cold water for emptying and cleaning housekeeping equipment.
No separation of clean supply storage from the soiled workroom, or a soiled workroom lacking the required work counter, clinical service sink and handwashing sink.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: 105 CMR 140.204 — Clean Storage and Soiled Holding Areas

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Hazardous and infectious waste not handled and disposed of properly by the clinic.
Semi-critical instruments and probes — those contacting mucous membranes or non-intact skin — given only a surface wipe rather than at least high-level disinfection, and not pre-cleaned first.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CDC Infection Prevention Checklist for Outpatient Settings, semi-critical item definition and High-Level Disinfection items A to C

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Shared non-critical devices used across a procedure day — blood pressure cuffs, chin rests, headrests, positioning equipment — not cleaned and disinfected between patients according to the degree of contamination.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CDC Infection Prevention Checklist, non-critical item definition; CDC Core Practices 5f

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Frequently touched surfaces in the procedure room not cleaned and then disinfected, and products not used to the manufacturer's dilution, storage, shelf-life and contact time.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: CDC Infection Prevention Checklist for Outpatient Settings, Section II items A and B

commonSurveyFindings.finding: No written schedule for cleaning and decontamination, and contaminated work surfaces not decontaminated after procedures, after spills, and at end of shift.
commonSurveyFindings.frequency: Federal OSHA cited 29 CFR 1910.1030 three times in one inspection of other health practitioners' offices between October 2024 and September 2025 — the most-cited standard for that sector.

_publishGate: Requires >=10 faqs and >=6 commonSurveyFindings before this service may go live. Survey findings are real regulatory citations — never invented, never inferred from the regulation text. Source: data/_todo-clinical-input.md.
